results.question: What is the process for submitting and evaluating the M.Tech dissertation (Part-B) at NIT Warangal?
results.ground_truth: Step 1: The student must complete all required courses before submission. Step 2: A plagiarism report (<20%) must be prepared along with three annexures per the Anti-plagiarism policy. Step 3: Three/four bound copies plus a soft copy must be submitted to the HoD within the Academic Calendar deadline. Step 4: A mid-term review is conducted by the evaluation committee (40 marks by guide + 20 marks mid-term review + 40 marks end-semester viva-voce). Step 5: The end-semester viva-voce is conducted by an external examiner along with an evaluation committee constituted by the HoD. Step 6: Grades are finalized by the committee.
results.answer:
The process for submitting and evaluating the M.Tech dissertation (Part-B) at NIT Warangal involves several steps. Students are required to submit a report on the dissertation work carried out by them, using a template that can be downloaded from the NIT Warangal website. The report should show that the plagiarism is within 20% along with three annexures as stipulated in the Anti-plagiarism policy. 

The evaluation of Dissertation Part-B is done for 100 marks, with the following weightages: 
* Periodic evaluation by Guide: 40 marks
* Midterm review: 20 marks
* End Semester viva-voce examination: 40 marks

The midterm review and the end semester viva-voce examination are conducted by a committee constituted by the Head of the Department. The student is required to submit three/four bound copies along with a soft copy of the dissertation to the Head of the Department/Centre within the last date prescribed in the Academic Calendar. 

If the performance of the student in Dissertation Part-B is not satisfactory, they will be awarded a 'U' grade and will have to repeat the dissertation work by registering in the next semester. They may be given an option to either continue with the same topic and guide or change the guide and topic of their dissertation.

results.question: What is the process for a B.Tech student to apply for temporary withdrawal from the institute?
results.ground_truth: Step 1: The student must apply to the institute through the Head of the Department within 15 days of the commencement of the semester OR from the last date of attending classes, whichever is later. Step 2: The application must state the full reasons for withdrawal, include supporting documents, and have endorsement from the parent/guardian. The institute is satisfied that, inclusive of the period of withdrawal, the student is likely to complete all the requirements for the degree within the time limits specified. There should be no outstanding dues against the student or demands from the student in the Institute/ Department/ Hostel / Library etc.

If a student applies for withdrawal before the start of the semester or within 15 days of the commencement of the semester, he/she will be exempted from paying the tuition fee for that semester. A student will be granted only one such temporary withdrawal during his/her tenure.

results.question: What is the step-by-step process for submitting a PhD thesis at NIT Warangal, from the pre-synopsis seminar to the award of degree?
results.ground_truth: 1) Ensure 2 publications as first author (SCI/SCIE). 2) Present Pre-Synopsis Seminar to DSC. 3) DSC certifies adequacy and DSC Chairman sends report to Dean-Academic. 4) Submit synopsis (max 10 pages) at least 1 month before thesis, with DSC certificate. 5) DSC recommends panel of 10 examiners (5 India, 5 abroad) to Senate Chairman. 6) Submit soft and hard copy of thesis within 3 months of synopsis submission (with plagiarism report, <20% similarity). 7) External examiners evaluate and submit reports. 8) Dean-Academic and Senate Chairman review reports. 9) Viva-voce board examination. 10) Senate recommends award to Board of Governors.
results.answer:
The step-by-step process for submitting a PhD thesis at NIT Warangal is as follows:

1. Pre-synopsis seminar: The scholar is required to present a pre-synopsis seminar to review their progress, at least one seminar per semester before the Doctoral Scrutiny Committee (DSC).
2. Submission of synopsis: The scholar submits their synopsis along with the required certificates, including a declaration by the Research Supervisor in a prescribed proforma (Ph.D. THESIS SUBMISSION CERTIFICATE).
3. Preparation of thesis: The scholar prepares their thesis, ensuring that it does not contain any work previously submitted for the award of any degree, and obtains an originality report generated by any plagiarism checking software suggested by the Institute.
4. Submission of thesis: The scholar submits their thesis, accompanied by the originality report and three annexures, to the Institute.
5. Evaluation by examiners: The thesis is evaluated by examiners, who can recommend that the thesis be accepted for the award of the PhD degree, subject to clarification of certain points at the time of viva-voce, or subject to minor modifications and correction of errors.
6. Viva-voce: The scholar presents their thesis in a viva-voce examination, where they clarify any points raised by the examiners.
7. Award of degree: If the thesis is accepted, the scholar is awarded the PhD degree.

Note: The exact timeline and specific requirements may vary, but the above steps provide a general outline of the process.

(Source: PhD_Regulations_(2024-25)_2024-7-17-11-38-22)

Part-2 involves a seminar presentation by the scholar on the state of the art in the proposed research area, along with objectives and methodology, to the Comprehensive Examination Committee.
